page: Amazon Web Services r7a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 48 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The memory configuration consists of 384.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 RAM, translating to 8.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks highlight the strong capabilities of the AMD EPYC processor and DDR5 memory subsystem. The instance achieves top-tier results in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core scores, and software compilation with Clang. Memory bandwidth for cached small blocks also ranks in the top tier, while uncached large-block read and write speeds remain strong. In database operations, the server demonstrates top-tier efficiency with Redis SET operations and strong PassMark database scores. LLM inference speeds are strong for small to medium models, though performance drops to average for large 70B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r7a.12xlarge offers a dense memory-to-CPU ratio suitable for memory-intensive enterprise applications without the overhead of GPU hardware. However, the lack of local NVMe storage means all storage operations must traverse the network, which may introduce latency compared to storage-optimized instances. This server is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput web serving, software compilation pipelines, and medium-scale data processing workloads.
description: The Amazon Web Services r7a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 384.0 GB of 5 GHz DDR5 RAM, delivering 8.0 GB of memory per core. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but features a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, software compilation speeds, and cached memory bandwidth, alongside strong database and Redis SET operations. This hardware profile makes the r7a.12xlarge highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, large-scale web serving, and software development pipelines, though the absence of local storage requires reliance on network-attached volumes.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 19 Gbps network baseline, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 48 AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized platform featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and DDR5 RAM for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
384.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier multi-core CPU and cached memory bandwidth performance
19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Optimized for in-memory databases, Redis, and software compilation
